62f2d45f2ad3e45a654f2bd5.question: It's not Lakmé, but during a Flower Duet in this opera, the soprano and her mezzo servant pluck every flower in the garden to decorate a house overlooking the harbor. The title 15-year-old soprano in this opera says that her lover will call her "little wife" and "scent of verbena" as she waits for a thread of smoke from a naval ship in "Un bel di, vedremo." That aria is delivered to allay Suzuki's doubts by a woman who calls herself "Madama B. F. Pinkerton." For 10 points, identify this opera by Giacomo Puccini in which Cio-Cio San ("cho cho san") commits seppuku.
